rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page. Very legible and unambiguous.

rows.description: Domus Fund payment on acct of
rows.notes: Clear header beneath the year. The handwriting reads as 'Domus Fund payment on acct of' (acct = account) with small uncertainty on the abbreviation interpretation.

Top-right boxed/carried amount reads 'Brought for'd' with numerals interpreted as 2045 2 2. The hundreds and thousands digits are clear, but the final two small digits (shillings/pence) are slightly smudged — chosen reading 2s 2d as the most plausible alignment.
Amount written as '17.3 1/2' — interpreted as £17 3s and a halfpenny. The halfpenny symbol is clearly a small loop/'ob' after the pence column; alignment with vertical rules is unambiguous.
Clear notation '1.1.8' in the three currency columns; digits are distinct and all within valid ranges.
Shows '1.18.11 1/2' — read as £1 18s 11½d. The halfpenny symbol after the pence is visible; shilling value 18 is clear and valid (<20).
Two numerals aligned in pounds and shillings columns read '13 13' with no pence shown. Handwriting legible; no fraction noted.
Amount reads '3 3' aligned in pounds and shillings; pence column blank. Clear and unambiguous.
Clear three-column amount '21 2 9' (£21 2s 9d). Alignment with neighbouring rows confirms column boundaries.
This line introduces a small group (sub-items follow with dashes). The handwriting is somewhat abbreviated ('vic &c') and slightly smudged; no amount appears on the same line.
Sub-item of the Kidlington repairs group, shown with a dash. The number '25' aligns in the pounds column; shillings and pence blank. Interpretation as £25 0s 0d is most consistent with alignment.
Reads '30' in the pounds column as another sub-item (do = ditto). Clear alignment; no pence/shillings shown.
Another sub-item with '30' in the pounds column. Legible and consistent with previous two sub-items.

Handwritten description is hard to read; I transcribe it as 'Salucati' but this is uncertain. The numerals at right look like '310 15' (interpreted as £310 15s 0d). This interpretation keeps pence within valid range; however the reading of the thousands/hundreds boundary and the exact description are uncertain.
Clear '73 8 9' adjacent to the word 'Rector'. Digits are well-formed and columns align cleanly.
Description partially unclear (read as 'Lewis. Melton Channel'). Amount reads '3 16 6' and columns are legible. Minor uncertainty in the name only.
Reads '15 6 6' next to a line that appears to be 'Downs Wine Bottles' (handwriting slightly cramped). Amount columns are clear.
Row uses 'do. do do' (ditto style). Amount '15 2 6' visible; interpretation of repeated 'do.' is straightforward but handwriting is slightly less crisp than above.
Description and amount read '25' in pounds column. The name is a little faded but legible enough to match common parish ledger names. No shillings/pence shown.
Amount '42 7 7' is clearly aligned in the three currency columns. Description legible.
Reads '22 10' with no pence. The two column digits (pounds and shillings) align well and are clear.
Amount appears as '7 7 4' (7£ 7s 4d). Handwriting slightly fainter here, but digits are still distinct and within valid ranges.
Reads '14 9' with blank pence column. The two digits align clearly with pounds and shillings columns.
The name 'Hill' is legible; the adjacent numeral looks like '18' in pounds column but is fainter than nearby rows, so pounds-only interpretation chosen with moderate confidence.
Clearly reads '3 18 3' next to 'Robey'. All three currency digits visible and valid.
Amount reads '13 2' with pence blank. Alignment consistent with pounds/shillings columns.
Second occurrence of 'Hill' with amount '12 6' — clear in pounds and shillings columns; pence blank.
Labeled 'Do' (ditto). Amount '10 3' visible but somewhat faint; interpreted as £10 3s 0d based on alignment.
Reads '10 9' adjacent to 'Brown'. Numerals clear in pounds and shillings columns.
Small numeral '1' is present next to 'Painting' but is faint. Interpreted as £1 0s 0d (no shillings/pence indicated).
Reads '6 6' aligned under the shillings and pence columns (no pounds). Interpreted as £0 6s 6d. The abbreviation 'Comm'n' is legible as commission.
Reads '2 0 11' next to 'Robey' below the cottages line. Alignment shows 2 in pounds, 0 in shillings, 11 in pence — all digits are legible.
Small '5' visible beside 'Chancel Williams' but slightly faint; interpreted as £5 0s 0d. The description is legible though with minor flourish on the name.
Reads '29 14 6' clearly. The descriptor 'local Rate fund (auction book)' is somewhat condensed but the words are readable and the amount columns are distinct.
Single numeral '15' appears on the shillings line next to 'Stamps' (no pounds shown). Slight uncertainty whether this is 15s or 15 (pounds); alignment indicates 15s is most likely.

Description is uncertain ('Stringer Comm'n on a Pike' is a best reading of cramped handwriting). The nearby numerals read about '9 6' under shillings/pence (no pounds) — interpreted as £0 9s 6d. This row is of moderate-low confidence due to ambiguous text and cramped digits.
Reads '6 6' in shillings/pence columns adjacent to text about room rent overpayment. Columns align clearly and handwriting for the numerals is crisp.
Bottom boxed total clearly reads '2774 7 4 1/2' (interpreted as £2774 7s 4½d). Boxed total is well-aligned and serves as the page subtotal; digits and the halfpenny symbol are distinct.
